title: Passport and travel document fee changes - Canada.ca
content:
## Passport and travel document fee changes
As of March 31, 2026, most passport and travel document fees increased to reflect inflation and service delivery costs. These fees will adjust annually in accordance with the Service Fees Act.
If we receive your application on or after March 31, 2026 , you will need to pay the new fee.
- Paying the correct fees
The following fees increased on March 31, 2026.
Old fee (CAN$) Before March 31, 2026
Before March 31, 2026
New fee (CAN$) On or after March 31, 2026
On or after March 31, 2026
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up) Passport fee for Canadians living in Canada
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
Passport fee for Canadians living in Canada
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up) Passport fee for Canadians living in Canada
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
Passport fee for Canadians living in Canada
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up) Passport fee for Canadians living outside Canada
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
Passport fee for Canadians living outside Canada
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up) Passport fee for Canadians living outside Canada
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
Passport fee for Canadians living outside Canada
Child passport (5-year validity only) Passport fee for Canadians living in Canada
Child passport (5-year validity only)
Passport fee for Canadians living in Canada
Child passport (5-year validity only) Passport fee for Canadians living outside Canada
Child passport (5-year validity only)
Passport fee for Canadians living outside Canada
Adult certificate of identity (age 16 and up)
Adult refugee travel document (age 16 and up)
Weekend or statutory holiday service

## Paying the correct fees

### Online and in-person applications
When you apply online or in person, the amount you pay is based on the day you submit your application.
Before you submit it, check that you paid the correct amount.
If you mail us your application, there’s a delay between when you send it and when we receive it. Fees may have changed while your application was in transit.
The fee you must pay depends on the date we receive your application, not the date you mail it .

#### What happens if you paid the old fee
The next steps depend on when we receive your application.
During this transition period, we will continue to accept applications that include the old fee.
You do not need to pay the fee difference if all of the following apply:
- We receive your application on or before April 29, 2026.
- You applied using an application form that lists the old fees.
- Your application is complete, including application form photos all your supporting documents fees
- all your supporting documents
We may send your application back to you and ask that you submit it again with the correct fees.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
